ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Arabic translation means 'Hosting header logo' instead of 'Logo for the portal'. It introduces the concept of 'hosting' and 'header' which are not present in the source.
The Azerbaijani translation means 'Logo for the section header' instead of 'Logo for the portal'.
The Bulgarian translation adds extra information ('for the space header') that is not present in the English source, changing the scope of the text.
The Czech translation refers to the 'header of the space' (záhlaví prostoru) instead of the 'portal' (portál).
The German translation means 'Logo for the area header' instead of 'Logo for the portal'.
The Greek translation adds 'της κεφαλίδας του' (of the header), which is not present in the English source. The English text is simply 'Logo for the portal'.
The Spanish translation refers to the 'header of the space' (encabezado del espacio) instead of the 'portal' (portal).
The French translation adds 'de l'en-tête' (of the header), which is not present in the English source. The English text refers to the 'portal', not the 'header'.
The Italian translation refers to the 'header of the space' (intestazione dello spazio) instead of the 'portal' (portale).
The Japanese translation adds 'header' (ヘッダー用), which is not present in the English source. The English text simply says 'Logo for the portal'.
The Korean translation '스페이스 헤더 로고' means 'Space header logo', which is not the same as 'Logo for the portal'.
The Lao translation means 'Logo for editing the portal header' instead of 'Logo for the portal'. It adds unnecessary information about 'editing the header'.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: ໂລໂກ້ສຳລັບພໍຕອນ

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Latvian translation adds 'galvenei' (for the header), which is not present in the English source. The English text is a simple description of the object, whereas the Latvian specifies its location.

The Dutch translation adds 'van de ruimte' (of the space) and 'koptekst' (header), which are not present in the English source. The English source specifically says 'portal', while the Dutch says 'header of the space'.
The Polish translation adds 'nagłówka' (header), which is not present in the English source. The English text refers to the portal logo, not specifically the header logo.
The translation adds 'do cabeçalho do espaço' (of the space header), which is not present in the source text. The source only specifies 'for the portal'.
The translation adds 'do cabeçalho do espaço' (of the space header), which is not present in the source text. The source only specifies 'for the portal'.
The Romanian translation adds 'antetul spațiului' (space header), which is not present in the English source 'Logo for the portal'.
The Russian translation adds 'шапки' (header), which is not present in the English source. The English text is a general description, while the Russian specifies a location.
The Slovak translation translates 'portal' as 'hlavičku priestoru' (header of the space), which changes the meaning from 'portal logo' to 'header logo'.
The Slovenian translation adds 'glavo' (header), which is not present in the English source. The English text refers to the 'portal', while the Slovenian text refers to the 'header of the space'.
The translation 'Logo për kokën e hapësirës' literally means 'Logo for the head of the space', which is a nonsensical translation for 'Logo for the portal'.
The Serbian translation means 'Logo for spacious header' instead of 'Logo for the portal'.
The Serbian translation means 'Logo for the space header' instead of 'Logo for the portal'.
The Ukrainian translation adds 'заголовка' (header), which is not present in the English source. The English text refers to the portal logo, not specifically a header logo.
The Vietnamese translation 'Logo cho tiêu đề không gian' means 'Logo for the space header', which is not the same as 'Logo for the portal'.
The translation adds '协作空间的标头' (Workspace header), which is not present in the source English text.
